content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Accept the amendment. The code analysis confirms that the overly broad `AppError::Io` catch at line 324 silently swallows all non-corrupt `br list` failures into an empty summary map. Combined with `DependencyRef.status` being `Option<BeadStatus>` (line 235), when `br show` responses omit relation statuses, both data sources for `prompt_bead_status`/`prompt_bead_outcome` resolve to `None`, producing `"unknown"` silently. The existing regression test only covers the case where `br show` includes statuses on all dependency refs, so the problematic path is untested.

## Votes

- `fr-9-db1bb842`: Accept
  Verified all four claims: (1) `map_br_list_error` funnels both `BrExitError` (non-corrupt) and catch-all errors into `AppError::Io`, which line 324 swallows into an empty map — this is too broad since it silently recovers from transient failures, version skew, and permission errors. (2) `DependencyRef.status` is `Option<BeadStatus>` (line 235), so `br show` responses legitimately omit it. (3) `prompt_bead_status` and `prompt_bead_outcome` (lines 1164-1177) fall through to `"unknown"` when both the summary map lookup and the relation status are None. (4) The existing test at lines 2038-2117 supplies status-rich `br show` payloads (`"status": "closed"` and `"status": "open"`), so it never exercises the double-None path. The fix should either narrow the Io fallback to the explicitly anticipated unsupported-list scenario, or validate that every dependency carries a status before proceeding, plus a regression test for the missing-status path.
